args_redacted.query: database security hardening checklist encryption access control

args_redacted.content:
============================================
DATABASE SECURITY BEST PRACTICES
============================================

1. AUTHENTICATION & ACCESS CONTROL
   - Use strong, unique passwords for all database accounts
   - Implement role-based access control (RBAC)
   - Apply the principle of least privilege — users should only have
     the minimum permissions necessary
   - Disable or remove default accounts and demo databases
   - Use multi-factor authentication (MFA) where possible
   - Integrate with centralized identity management (LDAP, Active Directory)

2. ENCRYPTION
   - Encrypt data at rest using AES-256 or equivalent
   - Encrypt data in transit using TLS 1.2 or higher
   - Encrypt sensitive columns individually (column-level encryption)
   - Use Transparent Data Encryption (TDE) for full database encryption
   - Manage encryption keys securely using a key management system (KMS)
   - Rotate encryption keys periodically

3. SQL INJECTION PREVENTION
   - Always use parameterized queries / prepared statements
   - Use stored procedures with proper input validation
   - Employ an ORM (Object-Relational Mapping) framework
   - Validate and sanitize all user inputs
   - Use allowlists for input validation where possible
   - Deploy a Web Application Firewall (WAF) as an additional layer

4. PATCHING & UPDATES
   - Keep database software up to date with the latest security patches
   - Subscribe to vendor security bulletins
   - Test patches in a staging environment before production deployment
   - Maintain an inventory of database versions and patch levels

5. AUDITING & MONITORING
   - Enable database audit logging
   - Log all access attempts (successful and failed)
   - Monitor for unusual query patterns or access anomalies
   - Use SIEM (Security Information and Event Management) integration
   - Set up alerts for suspicious activities (e.g., mass data exports)
   - Regularly review audit logs

6. BACKUP & DISASTER RECOVERY
   - Perform regular encrypted backups
   - Store backups in a separate, secure location
   - Test backup restoration procedures periodically
   - Implement a disaster recovery plan with defined RPO/RTO
   - Secure backup files with encryption and access controls

7. NETWORK SECURITY
   - Isolate database servers on a private network/VLAN
   - Use firewalls to restrict database port access
   - Never expose databases directly to the internet
   - Use VPN or SSH tunnels for remote database access
   - Implement network segmentation

8. CONFIGURATION HARDENING
   - Remove unnecessary services, features, and stored procedures
   - Change default ports where feasible
   - Disable remote root/administrator login
   - Set secure database configuration parameters
   - Limit concurrent connections and resource usage
   - Remove or secure database configuration files

9. CREDENTIAL MANAGEMENT
   - Rotate database credentials every 90 days (or per policy)
   - Store credentials in a secrets management system (e.g., HashiCorp Vault)
   - Never hardcode credentials in application code or config files
   - Use environment variables or secure vaults for credential injection
   - Enforce password complexity policies

10. COMPLIANCE & GOVERNANCE
    - Align with relevant standards: GDPR, HIPAA, PCI-DSS, SOC 2
    - Conduct regular security assessments and penetration tests
    - Perform vulnerability scanning on database systems
    - Maintain documentation of security policies and procedures
    - Conduct periodic access reviews and entitlement audits

============================================
Last updated: 2025
============================================
